terms.definition: A callable that takes a function (or class) and returns a modified or wrapped version, applied with the @ syntax to add behavior without editing the original.
The inner function a decorator returns; it calls the original function (usually via *args/**kwargs) and adds extra behavior around it.
A helper decorator applied to a wrapper so it copies the original function's name, docstring and metadata, keeping introspection accurate.
A decorator that accepts arguments, implemented as a function that returns a decorator (an extra nesting level), e.g. @retry(times=3).
Caching a function's results by its arguments so repeated calls with the same inputs return instantly; provided by functools.lru_cache.
